objects.sourceSection: DOC-009 §18.1 (create table artifacts) — a RATIFIED, field-complete source. Corroborated by DOC-009 §18.3 (artifact corrections create 'new artifact; new semantic version; supersession link') and DOC-002 §4 (ARTIFACT is a member of the ratified ProfessionalWorkObjectType union).
objects.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-009 §18.1 on 2026-07-16, replacing 'UNSPECIFIED in BOTH DOC-002 and DOC-007 ... do not fabricate. OPEN ITEM.' That note was TRUE about DOC-002/DOC-007 and WRONG as a conclusion: it searched the meaning doc and the wire doc, never the STORAGE doc. DOC-009 §18.1 defines the artifacts table in full, and its primary key is 'references professional_work_objects(id)' — so at the ratified persistence plane the Artifact IS a Professional Work Object, carrying the envelope's semanticVersion. These 11 fields are that table, column-for-column; nothing here is designed. NOT ratified at the WIRE boundary: DOC-007 gives ARTIFACT no interface, no §5.2 id prefix, no command and no event, while THREE of its field types (outputArtifactIds, ArtifactReference, ArtifactRequirement) point at it — a dangling reference DOC-009 resolves and DOC-007 has not caught up to (§16 item 6, first-slice). Value domains deliberately left as string: DOC-009 types artifactType/status/securityClassification/retentionClass as bare 'text not null' with no CHECK constraint and no enum anywhere in the corpus. Inventing enum members here would be the fabrication the original note rightly forbade.

fields.note: DOC-009 §18.1 'artifact_type text not null'. NOT an enum — no value domain is ratified anywhere; do not invent one.
DOC-009 §18.1 'media_type text not null'. IANA media type.
DOC-009 §18.1 'storage_provider text not null'. §18.2 names candidates (workspace-local, object storage, S3-compatible, Azure Blob, GCS) as deployment choices, NOT as a closed enum.
DOC-009 §18.1 'storage_key text not null'. Part of uq_artifact_content (storage_provider, storage_key, content_hash).
DOC-009 §18.1 'content_hash text not null'. DOC-002 §31.1: 'Preserve content hashes for evidence and artifacts.' This is what makes §18.3 immutability checkable.
DOC-009 §18.1 'byte_size bigint' — the ONLY nullable non-FK column, so optional here.
DOC-009 §18.1 'producing_pwu_id text references professional_work_units(id)' — nullable. The §29.2 traceability chain's 'PWU → produces → Artifact' edge.

DOC-009 §18.1 'producing_execution_attempt_id text references execution_attempts(id)' — nullable. This is guide §8.4's 'producing Attempt/invocation' binding. NOTE: the corpus attributes an artifact to the PWU and the ATTEMPT — never to the Execution Step.
DOC-009 §18.1 'security_classification text not null'; enforced by §18.4. NOT an enum — no value domain ratified.
DOC-009 §18.1 'retention_class text not null'; enforced by §18.4. NOT an enum — no value domain ratified.

DOC-009 §18.1 'status text not null'. NOT an enum and NOT reused from EvidenceStatus — no artifact status machine exists in the corpus (DOC-002 defines no Artifact state machine). Do not borrow another object's lifecycle.

DOC-002 §4/§7.1: reusable PWU template owned by a published PWA version

fields.note: RPH-CON-009 (DOC-008 L402-417): type identity binds to a VERSIONED PWA, not a bare pwaId. Derived by definePwuType from the owning PWA's `version` (stable DRAFT->PUBLISHED; publishPwa does not change it). The ProposePwu CON-009 gate matches this against the Undertaking's bound pwaVersion, so a type from a DIFFERENT version of the same PWA cannot realize into this Undertaking. AUTHORED 2026-07-18 under the sponsor grant.

Per-child composition rules (cardinality + optional applicability). permittedChildTypeIds is the flat id projection of these; a child with no rule defaults to M1. Optional/additive — a type may carry only the flat projection.

JAN-PRPWA-DS-001 STD-2 (R-9): where this type's work is discharged. Optional/additive — absent resolves to INTERNAL, so pre-existing types are unaffected. DELEGATED_EXTERNAL ⇒ terminal (INV-1: no permitted child types) plus a boundaryContract (STD-3). Authored PER TYPE, never a global PWA setting (forward-compat F-1). The handler defaults it to INTERNAL on every write.

JAN-PRPWA-DS-001 STD-3 (R-9): the org-boundary ICD authored in lieu of decomposition. Present iff executionBoundary is DELEGATED_EXTERNAL (INV-1); the handler enforces coherence both ways (present-when-delegated, absent-when-internal) at the write boundary.

objects.note: DOC-002 §3: concrete instantiation bound to one PWA version; owns the Professional Work Graph
The durable, event-sourced transcript of the authoring agent's work on a DRAFT PWA — critical domain state (a precursor to the JanumiCode v2 governed stream), not UI metadata.

AUTHORED 2026-07-19 (JAN-IRP capability C7). The DURABLE first-class RPH coordination object: the harness that frames, plans, allocates, coordinates, waits, synthesizes, and escalates across PWUs and child harnesses. Before this, the RPH was realized only conceptually (a program coordinating PWUs via decomposition + execution plans) with no durable identity/state — so C7's headline proof obligation 'the RPH survives restart while waiting' was unprovable. This is the minimal durable plane; allocation/tactic-change/synthesis-queue are follow-on increments. Extends ObjectEnvelope.

UNRATIFIED-AUTHORED (annotated 2026-07-22 under sponsor grant, JAN-EXECPLAN DWP-07 remediation): set by PruneExecutionStep when this step reached SKIPPED because the plan's own branch logic EXCLUDED it, rather than by an operator waiver (SkipExecutionStep). The ratified StepState enum has 10 values and no PRUNED state, and SKIPPED is deliberately terminal-SUCCESS so a WAIVED skip lets the plan continue — so stepState alone cannot carry the distinction, and without it a pruned step's out-edges SATISFIED the rest of its own dead arm, resurrecting the excluded branch as startable work. This flag is the discriminator the flow gate reads (transition-gate.ts inEdgeDisposition / isDeadForPruning). Ratification pending. Cited: DOC-002 §21 (StepState), §21.1 (canSkipStep).

helperSubTypes.note: RATIFIED AFTER ALL — audit 2026-07-16 (AUDIT-placeholder-helpers.md). DOC-004 §5.1 (L267) contains `interface ApplicabilityRule { ... }` defining: objectTypeConditions, pwuKindConditions, lifecycleTriggers, eventTriggers, riskConditions, semanticChangeConditions, requiredTags, excludedTags, expression. The previous note said 'NOT field-defined ... Source TBD' WHILE CITING THIS SECTION — the harvest pass reached inside this very interface, took its enums, and recorded its fields as nonexistent. A pass that extracts one kind of thing reports the absence of every other kind. STILL A PLACEHOLDER IN CODE: the single '(undefined)' field below keeps gen-objects.ts emitting z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) — 'any object'. TRANSCRIPTION CAVEAT: 4 of 9 resolvable; ObjectTypeCondition/RiskCondition/SemanticChangeCondition/PolicyExpression all undefined. NOT fixed here: tightening this breaks live literals and needs an explicit migration, not a sweep.

helperSubTypes.name: ExecutionTransition
helperSubTypes.note: AUTHORED 2026-07-21 UNRATIFIED-AUTHORED under JAN-EXECPLAN-DR-004 (Tier 3C-ii), DERIVED from the ratified DOC-009 §10.3 execution_transitions columns (id, execution_plan_id, source_step_id?, target_step_id?, condition_expression jsonb?, transition_type). A guarded directed EDGE over an ExecutionPlan's steps: sourceStepId absent = a plan-entry edge, targetStepId absent = an exit edge; conditionExpression stays OPAQUE at the contract layer (the §10.3 jsonb envelope, z.unknown) and is validated against the hand-authored ConditionExpression grammar (rph-domain) at proposeExecutionPlan — the object-schema generator cannot emit that union (DR-004 §19-B1). transitionType is the EDGE ROLE (SEQUENTIAL|CONDITIONAL); the FLOW SEMANTICS (BRANCH/WAIT/PARALLEL_GROUP) live on the ratified ExecutionStep.stepType (RPH-DOC-002 §21), NOT the edge. DOC-007 schematizes no ExecutionTransition interface (§16 item 6). Ratification pending.

fields.note: id-ref (idPrefix 'trans')
id-ref: the owning ExecutionPlan (§10.3 execution_plan_id)
id-ref: the edge's source ExecutionStep (§10.3 source_step_id, NULLABLE); absent = a plan-entry edge
id-ref: the edge's target ExecutionStep (§10.3 target_step_id, NULLABLE); absent = an exit edge
OPAQUE §10.3 condition_expression jsonb envelope (generates z.unknown); validated against the hand-authored ConditionExpression grammar (rph-domain) at proposeExecutionPlan (DR-004 §19-B1)
the EDGE ROLE: SEQUENTIAL (unconditional) | CONDITIONAL (carries conditionExpression). §10.3 transition_type NOT NULL. Node kinds live on ExecutionStep.stepType (§21).

DISCLOSED, not invented: §6.2's 8 prose bullets (L345-354) read like the intended CONTENT of an AdmissibilityRule but are prose, not a typed interface, so they are the likely author-intent anchor for a future ratification, NOT a field source.

helperSubTypes.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-004 §6.1 (L317-340) on 2026-07-17. The previous entry was a single '(undefined)' field that kept gen-objects.ts emitting z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) — 'any object' — WHILE its own note cited §6.1: the harvest pass reached inside this very interface, took its two enums (EvidenceCardinality, RequiredForDispositions), and recorded its fields as nonexistent. All 9 fields are now byte-exact. HONEST SPLIT: 7 of 9 resolve to concrete shapes (id, evidenceType, description, purpose, cardinality, requiredForDispositions, mayBeWaived); 2 nested helpers are UNDEFINED and stay permissive — admissibilityRules (AdmissibilityRule[]) and freshnessRule (FreshnessRule). Both DISCLOSED, neither invented; they emit z.array(z.unknown()) / z.unknown().optional() exactly like the shipped WaiverRule.revalidationTrigger:'PolicyExpression'. Tightening this helper only constrains ELEMENTS of requiredEvidence/optionalEvidence, both hardcoded [] today, so it is non-breaking.

helperSubTypes.name: AssessmentCriterion

fields.note: DOC-004 §7. Stable criterion id (FS-01-schema, RR-04-..., IC-05). Referenced by CriterionResult.criterionId (DOC-007 §20.1) and by a waiver's waivedCriterionId (DOC-004 §12.2).

DOC-004 §7 — REQUIRED, and the one field the pre-existing content could not supply: the invented {id, statement, mandatory} shape carried a single string. MIGRATION RULE (2026-07-16), no invention: where the author wrote a 'Label: sentence' statement (12 of 41 seed criteria) the label becomes the name; otherwise the name is the id. A richer authoring surface should supply real names — that is content authoring, not schema transcription, and is NOT done here.
DOC-004 §7. The criterion statement. This is where the invented `statement` field's content goes.
DOC-004 §7: BOOLEAN|ENUMERATED|QUALITATIVE|QUANTITATIVE|COMPOSITE. The enum was ALREADY harvested out of this very interface — while the interface was recorded as 'NOT field-defined'.

DOC-004 §7: DETERMINISTIC|MODEL_JUDGMENT|HUMAN_JUDGMENT|HYBRID. Derivable from the policy's evaluator: the schema-invariant and identity-provenance floors are DETERMINISTIC; Reasoning Review is MODEL_JUDGMENT.
DOC-004 §7. id-ref[] into the policy's EvidenceRequirement list (§6.1). Empty where a policy declares no evidence requirements.

DOC-004 §7: INFORMATIONAL|ADVISORY|MATERIAL|BLOCKING|CRITICAL. THIS REPLACES THE INVENTED `mandatory: boolean` — a five-level ratified severity that had been collapsed to a Boolean, the same disease §16 item 12 names for waivers. MIGRATION, faithful to the live runtime: mandatory:true -> BLOCKING (assurance-rules.ts already maps both an open BLOCKING finding AND a mandatory NOT_MET criterion to REJECTED); mandatory:false -> ADVISORY (non-mandatory criteria are filtered out of the disposition entirely, so they must NOT be MATERIAL, which conditionally-satisfies).
DOC-004 §7. Pairs with CriterionResultOutcome's NOT_APPLICABLE (DOC-007 §20.1). False for the floor: §8.4's floor always applies to a material AI transformation.

helperSubTypes.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-004 §7 on 2026-07-16 (AUDIT-placeholder-helpers.md). The previous entry was a single '(undefined)' field noted 'NOT field-defined; DOC-004 §7 supplies criterionType/evaluationMethod/severityIfNotMet enums. Source TBD.' — CITING THE SECTION THAT DEFINES IT. Those three enums are MEMBERS of `interface AssessmentCriterion`; the harvest pass reached inside the interface, took its enums, and recorded its fields as nonexistent. All 8 fields are primitives or already-generated enums: ZERO undefined dependencies, the cleanest transcription of the nine ratified-but-placeholder helpers. WHY IT MATTERED: AssurancePolicy.criteria was z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())) = an array of ANY OBJECT, so the code invented {id, statement, mandatory} — no overlap with the ratified shape beyond `id` — and nothing could catch it. This is the mechanism of the hollow governed layer, closed at its highest-value point.

helperSubTypes.name: FindingDefinition

fields.note: DOC-004 §9.1. The finding code. RATIFIED PER POLICY as well: each policy's Findings section (§15.7, §16.5, §17.5, §18.5, §19.6, §20.4, §21.5, §22.5, §23.5, §24.5, §25.5, §26.5) lists its codes — 100 in total. Referenced at runtime by the floor gate's openFindingCodes and by a waiver's WaiverDetail.waivedCriterionId path.

DOC-004 §9.1. DERIVED MECHANICALLY from `code`, not authored: the finding codes ARE the names in SCREAMING_SNAKE (SOLUTION_SUBSTITUTION -> 'Solution substitution'). This is unlike AssessmentCriterion.name, where the ids ('FS-01-schema') carry no usable name and I authored 7 — disclosed in floor-policies.ts. Here the humanization is a formatting of existing content.
DOC-004 §9.1. Where the invented `statement` field's content goes.

DOC-004 §9.1: INFORMATIONAL|ADVISORY|MATERIAL|BLOCKING|CRITICAL — the same closed union as AssessmentCriterion.severityIfNotMet (§7). Direct rename of the invented `severity` field; values preserved exactly.

DOC-004 §9.1. ⚠️ AUTHORED, and the only genuinely judged field here. DOC-004 ratifies each policy's finding CODES but NEVER which claim types a given finding affects. Set to the finding's OWN POLICY's `evaluatedClaimTypes` — the widest reading the ratified model permits, and the only non-invented value available: a narrower per-finding subset would be authored judgment with no source at all. Nothing reads this field today (verified: zero readers repo-wide), so the risk is not wrong behaviour — it is a future reader believing a relationship that was inferred. Ratifying per-finding claim types is a sponsor decision.

DOC-004 §9.1. ⚠️ AUTHORED, same reasoning as affectedClaimTypes. Set to the finding's OWN POLICY's `permittedControlActions` — which for 4 of the 6 additive policies is now DOC-004's ratified set (§15.10/§17.8/§19.8/§23.7), so the value is structurally constrained by the model even though the per-finding default is not ratified. Nothing reads it today.

helperSubTypes.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-004 §9.1 on 2026-07-16. The previous entry was a single '(undefined)' field noted 'Referenced by AssurancePolicyDefinition.findingDefinitions. NOT field-defined; DOC-004 §9.1 supplies defaultSeverity + defaultControlActions + affectedClaimTypes. Source TBD.' — CITING THE SECTION THAT DEFINES IT, exactly like AssessmentCriterion (§7): the harvest pass took the enums out of the interface and recorded its fields as nonexistent. HONEST SPLIT of the 6 fields: 3 are TRANSCRIPTION (code; description<-statement; defaultSeverity<-severity), 1 is MECHANICAL (name, humanized from code), and 2 are AUTHORED (affectedClaimTypes, defaultControlActions — set to the owning policy's own sets; see their notes). findingDefinitions has ZERO readers repo-wide, so this is documentation-grade, not behaviour-grade. SEPARATE AND UNRESOLVED: the seed ships 11 of DOC-004's 100 ratified finding codes (6 policies ship zero of theirs). Whether the seeded library is a deliberate demo subset or is meant to be the catalog is a product question, not a schema one — surfaced, not decided.

helperSubTypes.name: DispositionRule

fields.note: DOC-004 §10.2 (L548-553). The 5-value Recommendation enum (SATISFIED|CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED|REJECTED|INCONCLUSIVE|ESCALATED) — deliberately NOT the 6-value AssuranceDisposition: a DispositionRule cannot itself yield WAIVED, which arrives only via the §12 waiver path.

DOC-004 §10.2 (L555). PolicyExpression is referenced 4× in DOC-004 (here, §6.1, §12.1, §13) but DEFINED NOWHERE in the corpus → z.unknown(). DISCLOSED, not invented — inventing a predicate grammar is the fabrication this audit exists to prevent.
DOC-004 §10.2 (L557). Optional. id-ref[] into the policy's EvidenceRequirement list (§6.1).
DOC-004 §10.2 (L558). Optional. Verbatim string[], NOT a closed AssuranceSeverity[] — the doc's own asymmetry (cf WaiverRule.prohibitedFindingSeverities); preserved, not 'improved'.
DOC-004 §10.2 (L559). Optional. 8-value IndependenceRequirementSchema (§8.1).

helperSubTypes.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-004 §10.2 (L547-560) on 2026-07-17. The previous single '(undefined)' field kept z.record while its own note cited §10.2 — the harvest pass took the two enums (AssuranceDispositionRecommendation, IndependenceRequirement) and recorded the fields as nonexistent. All 5 fields transcribed byte-exact; optionality is the doc's (disposition + condition required; the other three optional). 4 of 5 resolve to concrete shapes; `condition: PolicyExpression` is UNDEFINED in the corpus (referenced 4×, defined nowhere) and stays permissive z.unknown() — DISCLOSED, not invented. Two deliberate fidelity choices preserved: `disposition` is the 5-value Recommendation enum (WAIVED excluded — it arrives via the §12 waiver path), and `forbiddenOpenSeverities` stays verbatim string[], not AssuranceSeverity[]. Tightening only constrains ELEMENTS of dispositionRules, hardcoded [] today — non-breaking.

helperSubTypes.name: RemediationRule

fields.note: AUTHORED 2026-07-19 under the sponsor grant. The opaque residual predicate — exactly the family pattern of DispositionRule.condition / EscalationRule.trigger / WaiverRule.revalidationTrigger. PolicyExpression is UNDEFINED in the corpus, so it stays z.unknown() (DISCLOSED, not invented). The machine-evaluable scoping is appliesToFindingCodes / appliesToSeverities below.

AUTHORED 2026-07-19. The one RATIFIED building block — the remediation actions this rule prescribes, drawn from the §11 ControlActionSchema (23 values); DOC-003 §24 lists 'Remediation options' as a policy element and the per-policy §25-§35 'Control actions' subsections name these. Deliberately NOT called recommendedControlActions: that name is already taken by ValidatorResult.recommendedControlActions (a ControlActionRecommendation[] wrapper) — a collision. INVARIANT (§11), enforced NOW at AUTHORING — createAssurancePolicy / editAssurancePolicy reject a rule whose remediationActions are not ⊆ the policy's permittedControlActions (an explicit empty permitted set permits none, so any action rejects). Only remediation FIRING is staged, not this subset check.
AUTHORED 2026-07-19. Optional scope — the finding CODES this remediation applies to (absent = the policy's findings generally). Machine-evaluable against a recorded observation's findingCode.

AUTHORED 2026-07-19. Optional scope — the observation severities this remediation applies to. Verbatim string[], the same severity-as-string family asymmetry as DispositionRule.forbiddenOpenSeverities / WaiverRule.prohibitedFindingSeverities.

helperSubTypes.sourceSection: AUTHORED 2026-07-19 under the sponsor grant — the corpus defines no RemediationRule interface; grounded in ControlActionSchema (§11) + DOC-003 §24 'Remediation options' + the DispositionRule/EscalationRule/WaiverRule family pattern.
helperSubTypes.note: Replaces the FORCE_PLACEHOLDER z.record. The 14 ratified docs define RemediationRule NOWHERE, so unlike its 3 siblings (transcribed from a ratified interface) this shape is AUTHORED — grounded where the corpus speaks (ControlAction §11; the family pattern) and labelled AUTHORED where it is silent. SETTABLE via Create/EditAssurancePolicy (threaded like the other 5 rule arrays); runtime EVALUATION is STAGED — no remediation trigger point exists in the assurance loop yet (a remediation fires at a governed repair/decision step not built). The DOC-002 §17.1 (singular remediationPolicy on AssurancePolicy) vs DOC-004/DOC-007 (plural remediationRules[] on AssurancePolicyDefinition) whole-object divergence is INHERITED (the code already binds the plural definition form), not introduced here.

helperSubTypes.name: EscalationRule

fields.note: DOC-004 §13 (L669). PolicyExpression is UNDEFINED in the corpus → z.unknown(). DISCLOSED, not invented — the same dependency as DispositionRule.condition and WaiverRule.revalidationTrigger.
DOC-004 §13 (L671-678). The inline union byte-matches the 7-value EscalationTargetSchema (HUMAN_USER|PRODUCT_OWNER|ARCHITECT|SECURITY_REVIEWER|LEGAL_REVIEWER|SYSTEM_OWNER|INDEPENDENT_VALIDATOR).

DOC-004 §13 (L680). Verbatim string[]. The prose L685-697 ('the package should include: decision requested; subject; ...') describes package CONTENT as guidance, NOT a typed schema, so it is NOT upgraded to a helper.
DOC-004 §13 (L681). Optional. 23-value ControlActionSchema (§11).

AUTHORED 2026-07-19 under the sponsor grant — the machine-evaluable escalation trigger, mirroring how DispositionRule keeps forbiddenOpenSeverities alongside its opaque `condition`. §10.3 (L568-569) pairs the ESCALATED disposition with a CRITICAL open finding ('CRITICAL open finding → REJECTED or ESCALATED'); this names the open-observation severities that fire the escalation to escalationTarget. §10.3 is a DEFAULT precedence ('Unless a policy overrides it:'), and CRITICAL is the only severity that default pairs with ESCALATED (BLOCKING → REJECTED; MATERIAL → CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED / INCONCLUSIVE / REJECTED). This authored shortcut is RESTRICTED to that one case: createAssurancePolicy rejects any other value and Gate D (completeAssuranceAssessment) only acts on CRITICAL. It is NOT a claim the corpus forbids escalating another severity — §10.3 permits a policy to OVERRIDE the default, but that override is expressed through the ratified EscalationRule.trigger (§13), not this severity shortcut. Verbatim string[], the same severity-as-string family as DispositionRule.forbiddenOpenSeverities / WaiverRule.prohibitedFindingSeverities. Optional → escalationRules stays non-breaking; `trigger` remains the free-form residual for predicates not evaluable from assessment state.

helperSubTypes.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-004 §13 (L668-682) on 2026-07-17. The previous single '(undefined)' field kept z.record while citing §13 — the harvest pass took the two enums (EscalationTarget, ControlAction) and recorded the fields as nonexistent. All 4 fields transcribed byte-exact. 3 of 4 resolve to concrete shapes; `trigger: PolicyExpression` is UNDEFINED in the corpus and stays permissive z.unknown() — DISCLOSED, the same dependency as DispositionRule.condition / WaiverRule.revalidationTrigger; not invented. requiredPackage stays verbatim string[]: the prose L685-697 ('should include ...') is guidance for package content, not a typed shape. Tightening only constrains ELEMENTS of escalationRules, hardcoded [] today — non-breaking.

helperSubTypes.name: WaiverRule

fields.note: DOC-004 §12.1. THE RATIFIED HOME FOR THE FLOOR CONTENT-LOCK. `rejectIfFloorLocked` (assurance.ts) hand-rolls this today as a guard keyed to three hardcoded policy ids, citing INV-5 — a misattribution: INV-5 is `executionState=SUCCEEDED` MUST NOT imply `assuranceState=SATISFIED` (axis separation), nothing about waivability. A policy declaring `waiverAllowed: false` is the model's own way to say it. Wiring the guard to READ this is NOT done here (see the note on this helper).

DOC-004 §12.1. id-ref[] into this policy's own AssessmentCriterion ids (§7). The criterion-exactness the floor gate already enforces at runtime via WaiverDetail.waivedCriterionId (§12.2 'exact policy and criterion') — this is its POLICY-side declaration: which criteria may be waived at all.

DOC-004 §12.1. Transcribed as `string[]`, NOT AssuranceSeverity[] — §12.1 types it `string[]` and I am not 'improving' a ratified declaration. (§9.1's defaultSeverity IS a closed union; §12.1's is not. That asymmetry is the doc's, not a transcription error.)

DOC-004 §12.1. Pairs with §12.2's ratified 'authority' requirement, which the Decision envelope already carries. Typed `string`: §12.1 does not name AuthorityType, and DOC-002's AuthorityType enum is a different (adjacent) thing — binding them is a judgment, not a transcription.
DOC-004 §12.1 `maximumDuration?: string` — OPTIONAL. Bounds §12.2's 'duration or expiration', which WaiverDetail.expiresAt records per-waiver; this is the policy-side cap.
DOC-004 §12.1. §12.2 requires a waiver to record a rationale; this declares which fields of it a given policy demands.
DOC-004 §12.1 — OPTIONAL. §12.2's 'compensating controls', already carried per-waiver by WaiverDetail.compensatingControls (authored under the 2026-07-16 grant).

DOC-004 §12.1 `revalidationTrigger?: PolicyExpression` — OPTIONAL, and its type is the ONE undefined dependency: `PolicyExpression` is referenced 4x by DOC-004 and DEFINED NOWHERE in the 14-file corpus. It therefore emits as a permissive placeholder, which is correct and disclosed — inventing a predicate language here would be exactly the fabrication this audit exists to stop. 7 of 8 fields are real; this one waits for ratification.

helperSubTypes.note: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-004 §12.1 on 2026-07-16 (AUDIT-placeholder-helpers.md). The previous entry was a single '(undefined)' field noted 'Referenced by AssurancePolicyDefinition.waiverRules. NOT field-defined. Source TBD.' — DOC-004 §12.1 defines it in full. FREE TO TIGHTEN: `waiverRules: []` has exactly ONE writer (assurance.ts), so there are no instances to migrate; an empty array satisfies any element type. ⚠️ SHAPE ONLY — this does NOT retire `rejectIfFloorLocked`. Making the guard read `waiverAllowed` requires the store->runtime content path to be real first: today the seeded policy objects are never read (floor.ts hardcodes the plan), so a policy declaring waiverAllowed:false would be a field nothing consults — swapping a working hardcoded guard for a decorative one. The ratified home now EXISTS; occupying it is the next step, not this one.

fields.note: Referenced by AssuranceAssessment.rejectedEvidence (DOC-007 §19) and ValidatorResult.evidenceRejected (§20). NOT field-defined. Source TBD.

Referenced by AssuranceAssessment.recommendedControlActions (DOC-007 §19), ValidatorResult, and AssuranceAssessmentCompleted. validators/control-action-recommendation.schema.json listed in DOC-007 §3. Its .action field ∈ ControlActionSchema (23-value). Distinct from the ControlAction enum. Source from schema file.
Referenced by AssuranceAssessment.confidence (DOC-002 §18 ONLY; absent from DOC-007 §19). NOT field-defined. OPEN ITEM (retain or drop).

The origin class of the execution result, reusing the RATIFIED DOC-007 §7.1 ProvenanceRecord.originType enum verbatim (USER_INPUT|MODEL_GENERATION|TOOL_OUTPUT|HUMAN_DECISION|MIGRATION|DERIVED|IMPORTED). MODEL_GENERATION/TOOL_OUTPUT are AI/agent-produced — the authoritative signal §8.4 L841 needs and the floor gate reads as signal 0 (aiProduced). OPTIONAL (not required as on §7.1) so it is additive over existing {} construction sites; the gate falls through to its heuristics when absent. AUTHORED under §0.3 to fill the §16 item 23 withholding, grounded in §7.1 rather than invented.

The Execution Attempt that produced this result — the 'producing-Attempt/context binding' DOC-007 §16 item 23 withholds by name. Reuses the §7.1 ProvenanceRecord field name verbatim; keyed to DOC-009 §10.4 execution_attempts(id) (also the FK target of §18.1 artifacts.producing_execution_attempt_id). Optional. AUTHORED under §0.3.

The actor that PRODUCED this execution result (a step producer), typed by the ratified DOC-002 ActorReference (which already carries actorType HUMAN|AGENT|MODEL|... and modelId). A secondary aiProduced signal behind originType (actorType in {AGENT,MODEL}). Optional. Absorbs the de-facto {executedBy, modelId} step site — modelId lives inside ActorReference.

The actor that EVALUATED (a ValidatorResult.executionProvenance, DOC-007 §20). LOAD-BEARING: the only field READ today (assurance.ts, the INV-8 §39-inv-8 independence check compares it against the producer). Typed by the ratified ActorReference. Optional — spread only when present, so a strictObject must permit its absence.
Referenced by ValidatorResult.claimResults (§20). NOT field-defined; likely related to CriterionResult (§20.1). Source TBD. (Validator-payload type, not referenced by any of the 17 objects.)
Referenced by ValidatorResult.observations (§20). NOT field-defined; pre-persistence form of AssuranceObservation (§21). Source TBD. (Validator-payload type, not referenced by any of the 17 objects.)
USER | AGENT | SYSTEM — who produced this entry.
Render/semantic kind: message | thinking | tool_call | tool_result | error.
For tool_result entries: whether the proposed command was accepted.
The permitted child PWU Type id (matches an entry in the parent's permittedChildTypeIds projection).
M1 mandatory-exactly-one; M+ mandatory-one-or-more; C1 conditional-zero-or-one; C+ conditional-zero-or-more.
For conditional (C*) rules: free-text applicability condition. Structured predicate/resolver deferred (M7/M9/M11).

helperSubTypes.name: BoundaryContract
helperSubTypes.note: JAN-PRPWA-DS-001 STD-3: the interface-control document authored at an organizational boundary in lieu of an internal decomposition, for a DELEGATED_EXTERNAL PwuType. Self-contained (F-3) — counterparty + attested policies + optional note — so a future org-profile can rebind it without dangling cross-references. The required inputs/outputs exchanged with the counterparty reuse the PwuType's existing requiredInputs/requiredOutputs (the artifact hand-off plane). AUTHORED 2026-07-20 under the design-session sponsor grant.

fields.note: Human-readable identity of the external party discharging the work (e.g. 'Contract Lab — Hematology'). SHALL be non-empty; the min-length constraint is enforced at the handler write boundary (a strict schema cannot express minLength).

id-refs to ACTIVE non-floor AssurancePolicies the counterparty attests to satisfying (R-10, assurance-by-attestation). Each id present SHALL resolve to an ACTIVE non-floor policy, validated against real policy status at the broker (D-C Option 1); an empty list is permitted (a pure disclosure with no attested substitution — INV-2's substantive assurance is then unmet until attestation is captured at runtime, which is deferred). INV-5: this is a CLAIM by the counterparty, never authoring self-certification.
Free-text scope/condition of the delegation (e.g. 'STAT panels only; routine handled internally'). Structured applicability predicate deferred, consistent with PermittedChildRule.applicabilityNote.

helperSubTypes.name: WaiverDetail
helperSubTypes.note: AUTHORED under sponsor grant 2026-07-16 (not derived from a DOC-007 interface). Serializes DOC-004 §12.2's RATIFIED waiver invariants, which DOC-007 never schematized (§16 item 12: 'waiver lacks a complete instance/wire/storage contract'). Object+version+authority+rationale already live on the Decision envelope; these are the remainder.

fields.note: id-ref — the EXACT policy waived (DOC-004 §12.2 'exact policy and criterion').
The EXACT criterion waived. RPH-GOV-005: a waiver does not bleed to another criterion, object, or version.
id-ref[] — the finding(s)/observation(s) waived. §8.15: a waiver does not erase a finding; it remains visible.
RFC3339. Absent = no expiry. §8.15: expiration triggers review and may block promotion.
DOC-004 §12.2 'compensating controls'. Empty array = explicitly none.
id-ref[] — DOC-004 §12.2 'downstream impact'.
DOC-004 §12.2 'review conditions'.

conflicts.resolution: Both DOC-002 extractions claimed the envelope has NO schemaVersion and an EXTRA optional authorityId?. The ratified codebase (packages/rph-contracts/src/envelopes.ts objectEnvelopeShape) is authoritative and RESOLVES this: schemaVersion IS present; authorityId is DROPPED (explicit comment 'authorityId (DOC-002 §4) is DROPPED per docs §5'). The task-prompt envelope description (schemaVersion in, no authorityId) is correct. schemaVersion is one of the version quartet (contract/schema/semantic/revision).
DOC-002 §6.1 = required; DOC-007 §10.1 = optional (formalizedObjective?). DOC-007 (latest serialized) wins → optional.
DOC-002 §7.1 = 'boundary'; DOC-007 §11.1 = 'boundaries' (same WorkBoundary shape). DOC-007 wins → 'boundaries'.

decompositionContractId?, recompositionContractId?, currentBaselineId? appear in DOC-002 §7.1 but are ABSENT from DOC-007 §11.1. Per 'include every field from both' they are retained as OPTIONAL, flagged as open (DOC-007 may deliberately drop them).

conflicts.topic: ASSURANCE_POLICY envelope vs standalone + reshaped fields
conflicts.resolution: DOC-002 §17.1 models ASSURANCE_POLICY as extending ObjectEnvelope with fields targetTypes/applicabilityRule/requiredEvidenceRequirements/validatorRole/failureSeverity/remediationPolicy/escalationPolicy/waiverPolicy?. DOC-007 §17 defines a STANDALONE AssurancePolicyDefinition (own id/version/semanticVersion, no envelope) with a completely reshaped field set (applicableObjectTypes, applicability:ApplicabilityExpression, defaultClaimTemplates, requiredEvidence+optionalEvidence, criteria:AssessmentCriterion[], evaluatorRole, findingDefinitions, dispositionRules, permittedControlActions, remediationRules/escalationRules/waiverRules). policy-level failureSeverity is DROPPED (moves to FindingDefinition.defaultSeverity). DOC-007 wins. Note tension: ProfessionalWorkObjectTypeSchema still lists ASSURANCE_POLICY as an envelope objectType — see open items.

DOC-002 §18 field 'disposition' (AssuranceDispositionSchema, 6 terminal values) is REPLACED on the object by DOC-007 §19 'assessmentState' (AssuranceAssessmentStateSchema, 15-value state machine incl. VALIDATOR_FAILED/INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION). The 5-value terminal disposition (AssuranceDispositionRecommendationSchema) now lives on the AssuranceAssessmentCompleted event (§19.3), not the object. DOC-007 wins.

evaluator: DOC-002 required → DOC-007 optional (wins). recommendedControlAction? (single optional inline enum, DOC-002) → recommendedControlActions (required array of ControlActionRecommendation, DOC-007) (wins). startedAt/completedAt/confidence are DOC-002-only, retained-flagged.

DOC-002 §20.1 references by id array (stepIds: string[], transitionIds: string[]); DOC-007 §15 EMBEDS full object arrays (steps: ExecutionStep[], transitions: ExecutionTransition[]). DOC-007 wins → embedded.
DOC-002 §24.1 opaque itemObjectIds: string[]; DOC-007 §23 version-pinned itemObjectVersions: BaselineItemVersion[] ({objectId, semanticVersion, contentHash?}) + new purpose/scope. DOC-007 wins.

DOC-002 §17.1 listed 5 values (NONE|DIFFERENT_INVOCATION|DIFFERENT_AGENT|DIFFERENT_MODEL|HUMAN). Codebase IndependenceRequirementSchema (DOC-004 §8.1) is the authoritative 8-value superset (adds DIFFERENT_CONTEXT_INSTANCE, DIFFERENT_PROVIDER, ORGANIZATIONALLY_INDEPENDENT). Use the codebase schema.

conflicts.topic: Enums flagged 'not in provided list' actually exist
conflicts.resolution: The extractions flagged ActorType, AuthorityType, AssurancePolicyStatus, CriterionResult.result, and ValidatorResult.dispositionRecommendation as having no closed-enum schema. All DO exist in codebase enums.ts: ActorTypeSchema, AuthorityTypeSchema, AssurancePolicyStatusSchema, CriterionResultOutcomeSchema, and AssuranceDispositionRecommendationSchema (5-value, distinct from the 6-value AssuranceDispositionSchema). Wired accordingly.

Not a merge — two distinct closed enums. AssuranceSeveritySchema = 5 values (adds INFORMATIONAL); FailureSeveritySchema = 4 values. AssuranceObservation.severity → AssuranceSeveritySchema. (failureSeverity no longer appears at policy level in DOC-007.)

No conflict but load-bearing: Decision.authority uses ActorReference (both DOC-002 §23.1 and DOC-007 §22), NOT AuthorityReference. Only Obligation.authority and Constraint.authority use AuthorityReference.

openItems.item: RESOLVED 2026-07-16 — ARTIFACT's fields were defined all along, in DOC-009 §18.1
openItems.why: This item read: 'ARTIFACT is one of the 17 ProfessionalWorkObjectType values but neither DOC-002 nor DOC-007 defines an ArtifactObject interface/schema ... Fields are to-be-designed; do not fabricate.' Every clause about DOC-002 and DOC-007 is true. The conclusion was false: the search stopped at the meaning doc and the wire doc and never opened the STORAGE doc. DOC-009 §18.1 'create table artifacts' defines all 11 columns, and its PK 'references professional_work_objects(id)' makes the Artifact a Professional Work Object with an envelope semanticVersion — which §18.3 then versions under supersession. The fields are now TRANSCRIBED from that table, not designed. WHAT REMAINS OPEN is narrower and belongs to DOC-007, not to the object: no ARTIFACT interface, no §5.2 id prefix ('art' is authored here; DOC-004 §… writes 'artifact_01J...'), no command, no event — even though outputArtifactIds/ArtifactReference/ArtifactRequirement all point at it. GENERALIZE THIS: 'unspecified — do not fabricate' notes in this vocab were written against DOC-002+DOC-007 only. DOC-009 is a ratified source and must be checked before any such note is trusted; the same blind spot produced a false blocker on §16 item 23 (execution_attempts, which DOC-009 §10.4 likewise defines in full).

openItems.item: 6 objects lack a DOC-007 serialized field contract
openItems.why: AMENDED 2026-07-16: ARTIFACT is no longer sourceless — DOC-009 §18.1 defines it (see the resolved item above). It still lacks a DOC-007 SERIALIZED contract, so it stays in this list, but the gap is now wire-only, not total. The other five remain DOC-002-prose-only; each should be re-checked against DOC-009's storage schema before being called unspecified. ORIGINAL: OBLIGATION, CONSTRAINT, DECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT, RECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT, RUNTIME_BINDING (and ARTIFACT) have no field-level definition in the latest serialized contract (DOC-007). CONSTRAINT + RUNTIME_BINDING have schema FILES listed in DOC-007 §3 but no prose; the others have neither. All are sourced from DOC-002 prose and need serialization before codegen. Their enums (ObligationType/Strength/Status, ConstraintType/Strength/Status, CoverageType, DecompositionContractStatus, RecompositionContractStatus, AuthorizationStatus, StepType/State) exist but have no wired consumer field yet.

openItems.item: CORRECTED 2026-07-16 — ~34 helpers are placeholders, but 9 of them ARE ratified and field-complete
openItems.why: The original read: '~40 helper sub-types are referenced but not field-defined. Only 12 helpers are field-defined (... ApplicabilityExpression ... ValidatorResult ...)'. FALSE for 9 of 34, verified by a 34-way audit of the FULL 14-file corpus with adversarial refutation of every positive, then re-checked by hand (AUDIT-placeholder-helpers.md). DOC-004 defines SEVEN: AssessmentCriterion §7, FindingDefinition §9.1, DispositionRule §10.2, WaiverRule §12.1, EscalationRule §13, EvidenceRequirement §6.1, ApplicabilityRule §5.1 — all cited by the old notes as 'Source TBD'. DOC-007 defines TWO: ApplicabilityExpression §18, ValidatorResult §20 — and this list ALREADY names those two as field-defined; they are placeholders anyway because gen-objects.ts hardcodes FORCE_PLACEHOLDER for them, an M1 deferral in a repo past M14. CONSEQUENCE: every field that makes an AssurancePolicy mean anything (criteria, findingDefinitions, waiverRules, dispositionRules, escalationRules, requiredEvidence, applicability) is typed 'any object' — which is the MECHANISM behind the hollow governed layer, not a symptom of it. Proof: floor-policies.ts writes {id, statement, mandatory}; DOC-004 §7 ratifies neither 'statement' nor 'mandatory'. The 25 genuine negatives (incl. ExecutionProvenance) are now evidence-backed.

DOC-002 Constraint.applicability uses helper ApplicabilityRule; DOC-007 AssurancePolicyDefinition.applicability uses the field-defined ApplicabilityExpression (§18 op union). Ratify sheet C plans to unify them into one DSL (M7). Decide whether Constraint.applicability adopts ApplicabilityExpression.

openItems.item: ASSURANCE_POLICY: envelope PWO vs standalone definition
openItems.why: ProfessionalWorkObjectTypeSchema still enumerates ASSURANCE_POLICY as an envelope-extending objectType, but DOC-007 §17 models AssurancePolicyDefinition as standalone (own id/version/semanticVersion, no envelope). Confirm whether ASSURANCE_POLICY is a full PWO or a standalone definition object, and reconcile the objectType enum accordingly.

Outside the three extractions but load-bearing: the ratify sheet (A-3) retained an optional riskProfiles field on AssurancePolicyDefinition — required by DOC-004 §3.1, absent from DOC-007 §17 serialized (suspected unintended omission). Confirm it belongs, then add it as optional.

PWU.decompositionContractId?/recompositionContractId?/currentBaselineId? and AssuranceAssessment.startedAt/completedAt?/confidence? are present in DOC-002 but absent from the DOC-007 serialized contract. Retained (optional) per union rule but flagged — confirm keep vs drop for the serialized shape (startedAt/completedAt likely superseded by event-envelope occurredAt/recordedAt).

openItems.item: TraceLink and ExecutionStep are id-bearing but not PWO envelope objects
openItems.why: Both are field-defined and carry their own id (idPrefix 'trace' / 'step') but do NOT extend ObjectEnvelope and are NOT among the 17 ProfessionalWorkObjectType. Modeled here in the helper-sub-type catalog (not the 17-object list). ExecutionTransition and ExecutionStep also have persistence tables in §31.

openItems.item: ControlAction is an enum, not a helper object
openItems.why: DOC-007's helper listing tagged ControlAction as a referenced helper; it is actually the 23-value ControlActionSchema enum. AssurancePolicyDefinition.permittedControlActions is ControlAction[] with enumRef ControlActionSchema — excluded from the helper catalog. ControlActionRecommendation (whose .action ∈ ControlActionSchema) IS a distinct helper object and is retained.
